field.label: Production Environment
field.tooltip: Yes for live sites, No for dev/staging on non-standard domains.
field.comment:
Yes = this is your live site. The module checks the licence key against the current domain. If invalid, the module silently disables itself.
No = this is dev/staging. The module runs at full features without a licence — use this on non-standard dev domains the auto-detector misses.
Default: Yes.

field.label: License Key
field.tooltip: Paste your purchase-email key here. Bound to the production domain.
field.comment:
Paste the key from your purchase email. Tied to your live website's address.
Test or staging sites don't need a key — the module detects them automatically.
www. doesn't matter — one key works whether your address is shop.com or www.shop.com.

field.label: Enable Module
field.tooltip: Master on/off. When No, the Part Finder widget and vehicle filters do not render.
field.comment:
Master on/off switch.
Yes = the Part Finder widget renders and vehicle filters apply to catalog browsing.
No = the module does nothing on the storefront — useful for quickly disabling without uninstalling.

field.label: Earliest Year
field.tooltip: The oldest year that appears in the Year dropdown. Default 1990.
field.comment:
The oldest year that appears in the Year dropdown on the Part Finder.
Default 1990 covers modern cars.
Set to 1950 for vintage car parts shops (classic Mustangs, Land Rover Series, etc.).
Set to 2007 for smartphone-fitment shops where there's no point listing pre-iPhone years.
Anything below 1900 or above the current year is clamped to safe bounds.

field.label: Show Year Field
field.tooltip: Hide the Year dropdown if your fitment domain doesn't need it (phone cases, watches, etc.).
field.comment:
Show or hide the Year dropdown in the Part Finder form.
Yes (default) = Year dropdown renders — for any time-bound fitment (vehicles, electronics with model years, etc.).
No = Year dropdown is hidden — the form becomes Make → Model → Part. Useful for phone cases, watch straps, printer cartridges, appliance parts and similar domains where year doesn't matter.

field.label: "Make" Field Label
field.tooltip: Customer-facing label for the Make dropdown. Leave blank for the default ("Make").
field.comment:
Customer-facing label for the Make dropdown (top of the Part Finder).
Default: Make. Common alternatives:
- Brand — for phone cases, watches, appliances
- Manufacturer — for industrial parts
- Category — for cross-product fitment
Leave blank to use the default.

field.label: "Model" Field Label
field.tooltip: Customer-facing label for the Model dropdown.
field.comment:
Default: Model. Common alternatives:
- Phone / Tablet — for accessories
- Watch — for watch parts
- Model Number — for industrial parts

field.label: "Year" Field Label
field.tooltip: Customer-facing label for the Year dropdown.
field.comment:
Default: Year. Common alternatives:
- Generation — for phones (iPhone 13/14/15…)
- Year of Manufacture — for older fitments
- Model Year — for cars where it matters

field.label: "Parts" Field Label
field.tooltip: Customer-facing label for the Parts Required dropdown.
field.comment:
Default: Parts Required. Common alternatives:
- Type / Style — for phone cases
- Strap Size — for watch straps
- Component — for industrial parts

field.label: Show Fitment Badge on Product Page
field.tooltip: Yes = render a "Fits: BMW 3 Series 2018-2023" badge under the price on every PDP that has fitment data.
field.comment:
Renders a coloured "Fits: …" block right under the product price on the PDP for any product tagged with vehicle compatibility data.

Yes = badge renders when product has fitment data.
No (default) = no badge. Existing v1.0.x installs see no change unless they opt in.

field.label: Badge Prefix Text
field.tooltip: Bold label before the list of compatible vehicles. Default "Fits:".
field.comment:
Bold text shown before the vehicle list. Default Fits:. Alternatives:
- "Compatible with:" — formal
- "Made for:" — friendly
- "Bilingual stores": translate via Magento i18n.

field.label: Badge Style
field.tooltip: Visual treatment: success (green), info (blue), warning (amber), neutral (grey).
field.comment:
Success (green) — default, reads as a positive "yes this fits"
Info (blue) — neutral informational tone
Warning (amber) — attention-grabbing
Neutral (grey) — minimal

field.label: Enable SEO URLs
field.comment:
Adds a custom router that maps SEO-friendly paths to the Part Finder:
/parts/bmw/3-series/2020/brake-pads
instead of the default
/vehiclecompat/find/index?make=42&model=87&year=2020&part=12

Better SEO. Better social-share previews. Better crawlability.

Yes = both old and new URLs work — old links don't break.
No (default) = only the query-string URLs work. Existing v1.0.x installs see no change unless they opt in.

field.label: URL Prefix
field.tooltip: The first segment of the SEO URL. Default "parts". Use "fitment", "compat", "for", etc.
field.comment:
The first segment after the domain. Default: parts → /parts/bmw/3-series/…
Common alternatives:
- fitment → /fitment/bmw/…
- for → /for/bmw/… (very short, reads naturally)
- compatibility → longer but explicit

Sanitised to lowercase a–z, 0–9, and dashes. Anything else is stripped. Invalid values fall back to parts.

field.label: Enable Customer Garage
field.tooltip: Yes = customers can save their vehicle for one-click reload across sessions.
field.comment:
Adds a "My Garage" feature where customers can save the vehicle they picked in the Part Finder. Next time they visit, their vehicles are already there — one click reloads the Part Finder with that vehicle. Top-3 conversion driver for parts e-commerce.

v1.1.0 MVP: localStorage-based (per-browser). Guest + logged-in customer get the same experience.
v1.2.0+: customer attribute storage for logged-in users (syncs across devices).

Merchants place the widget where they want via layout XML or CMS block:
{{block class="ETechFlow\VehicleCompat\Block\Garage" template="ETechFlow_VehicleCompat::garage/widget.phtml"}}

No (default) = widget renders nothing even if placed.

field.label: Maximum Vehicles per Customer
field.tooltip: How many vehicles each customer can save. Default 3. Clamped 1-10.
field.comment:
How many vehicles each customer can save. Default 3.
Anything below 1 or above 10 is clamped to safe bounds. Most stores find 3 is the sweet spot — covers "my car, my wife's car, my work van".
